title: Implement nearest-first two-phase search in property-mounter.js
description: Replace the single-pass global DFS search in autoMountProperties() with a two-phase nearest-first strategy: Phase 1 searches the current node's subtree downward, Phase 2 walks upward through ancestors searching each ancestor's full subtree (excluding already-visited branches), stopping at the first ancestor level that yields a match. Also update scene-accessor.js to pass the current node instead of scene root.

criteria: autoMountProperties() accepts currentNode parameter and searches downward from it first
When no match found in current subtree, search walks upward through parent chain, searching each ancestor's full subtree excluding already-visited branches
Search stops at the first ancestor level that finds any match (does not continue to root if match found)
All 3 call sites in scene-accessor.js (lines ~450, ~510, ~563) pass the current node instead of cc.director.getScene()
Existing traverseNodeTree() reused for subtree searches with shared visited Set to avoid redundant visits
Safety limits preserved: maxDepth=50, maxNodes=5000 across both phases
Plugin loads without errors in Cocos Creator 2.x editor
convergence.verification: Load the plugin in Cocos Creator 2.x, add a component to a node with matching child/sibling/ancestor nodes, verify that the nearest matching node is auto-mounted first
convergence.definition_of_done: Auto-mount properties bind to the nearest matching node (searching outward from current node) instead of the first match found in global DFS order

changes: Rename rootNode parameter to currentNode in autoMountProperties() signature
Replace single traverseNodeTree(rootNode, callback) call with two-phase search logic per property
Phase 1: Call traverseNodeTree(currentNode, callback, visited) to search current node's subtree downward

Phase 2: If no bestMatch found, iterate upward via node.parent from currentNode; at each ancestor, call traverseNodeTree(ancestor, callback, visited) which skips already-visited nodes; if bestMatch found at this level, break
Share the visited Set across both phases so ancestor subtree searches skip the already-searched current branch
files.change: Transform autoMountProperties from global single-pass DFS to two-phase nearest-first search (down from current node, then up through ancestors with full subtree search at each level)

changes: Remove the visited.delete(nodeId) at line 55 so that visited Set persists across multiple traverseNodeTree calls within the same property search (needed for Phase 2 to skip already-visited subtrees)
files.change: Make visited Set persistent (remove cleanup of visited entries) so it can be shared across multiple traverseNodeTree calls for the same property

changes: Update the comment at line 449 from '搜索范围为当前场景' to reflect new nearest-first search strategy
files.change: Pass the current node (already available as `node` parameter) instead of scene root to autoMountProperties at all call sites

implementation: 1. In scene-accessor.js performAutoMount(), replace `const sceneRoot = cc.director.getScene()` with using the existing `node` parameter directly, passing it to autoMountProperties as the starting search node
2. In property-mounter.js, modify traverseNodeTree() to NOT delete visited entries (remove line 55 `visited.delete(nodeId)`) so visited Set persists across calls
3. In property-mounter.js autoMountProperties(), rename rootNode parameter to currentNode
4. Replace the single traverseNodeTree call per property with two-phase logic: Phase 1 - create fresh visited Set, call traverseNodeTree(currentNode, matchCallback, visited) for downward search

5. Phase 2 - if no bestMatch found, walk upward: let searchNode = currentNode.parent; while (searchNode) { call traverseNodeTree(searchNode, matchCallback, visited); if (bestMatch found) break; searchNode = searchNode.parent; }
6. Verify all 3 call sites in scene-accessor.js pass the correct node parameter
7. Test in Cocos Creator 2.x editor: add component to a node, verify nearest matching nodes are bound first

reference.pattern: Existing traverseNodeTree DFS pattern with visited Set and depth guards
reference.examples: The existing traverseNodeTree(rootNode, callback, visited, depth, maxDepth, maxNodes) can be called multiple times with the same visited Set to progressively expand search scope while skipping already-visited nodes

rationale.chosen_approach: Two-phase search reusing existing traverseNodeTree with shared visited Set - minimal code change, leverages existing infrastructure
alternatives_considered: Add proximity bonus to match scores (rejected: requires global search, slower, more complex scoring logic)
Create entirely new bidirectional traversal method (rejected: unnecessary complexity when existing traverseNodeTree can be composed)
Search current subtree then fall back to full scene search (rejected: user wants ancestor-by-ancestor expansion, not direct fallback to global)
rationale.tradeoffs: Removing visited.delete() changes traverseNodeTree behavior for any other callers, but currently only autoMountProperties uses it

manual_checks: Add component to leaf node - verify child nodes are matched before sibling/ancestor nodes
Add component to root node - verify behavior unchanged (only downward search possible)
Add component where no match exists in subtree - verify upward search finds ancestor-level matches
Add component where match exists at multiple ancestor levels - verify nearest ancestor level's match is chosen
Add component to multiple selected nodes simultaneously - verify each searches from its own position
success_metrics: Nearest matching node is auto-mounted (not global DFS-order first match)
Search stops at first ancestor level with match (does not search further ancestors)
No errors or warnings in editor console during auto-mount
Performance comparable or better than global search for typical scenes

regression: Verify auto-mount still works when component is added via panel UI (add-component handler)
Verify manual execute-auto-mount trigger works correctly
Verify mount config options (ignoreCase, flexibleMatching) still apply correctly
Verify showMountLog setting still shows correct log output

commit.message_template: feat(auto-mount): optimize search order to nearest-first strategy
